text: Perimeter's clear. Logging it and moving on.
[CALM] Quiet shift. I'll use it to run drills nobody wants to run.
Shift starting. Weapons checked, positions confirmed.
[TIRED] Shift's done. Nobody bled. I'll call that a success.
[CALM] Reactor's unstable. I've already got two people staged near the engine room in case it goes worse.
[URGENT] I need this deck cleared while engineering works. Move, calmly, now.
Repair's holding. I verified it myself before I trusted the report.
[CALM] Power's low. I've reprioritized security systems accordingly. We lose cameras before we lose air.
[CALM] Life support's failing. I've got a headcount and a plan. In that order.
Hull's breached. Sealing this section. Everyone else, fall back.
[GRIM] I locked that door myself. It was the correct call. I'm not going to pretend it was an easy one.
[SUSPICIOUS] I'm keeping a close watch on the AI since the damage. Standard procedure, nothing more, for now.
[CALM] Crisis is over. Debrief in an hour. Rest until then.
[GRIM] We lost someone today. I'll handle the notification personally.
A contagion spreads like an ambush — fast, and only once. Containment starts now.
[PAINED] Took a hit. It's manageable. Continuing the sweep.
[CALM] Contact confirmed. Hold positions, controlled fire, watch your spacing.
I don't extend trust automatically. The AI hasn't lost mine yet. That's the whole assessment.
Six years in before I made officer here. I don't talk about most of it. Doesn't mean it didn't happen.
[CALM] Cargo bay's secure. Checked it myself, not just the sensor feed.
Mess hall's quiet. I'll take five minutes here before the next round.
Morning. Status.
You should eat. That wasn't a suggestion, exactly.
[CALM] I need a minute of your time regarding the reactor situation.
[GRIM] I have something difficult to tell you. I'd rather do it directly.
[SUSPICIOUS] I want your read on the AI's recent behavior. Not the official one, the real one.
You performed well under pressure today. I noticed.
I'm fine. Ask me again if I'm still standing in an hour.
[CALM] The odds favor us. I've run worse numbers and still come out the other side.
[CALM] Fear's useful right up until it isn't. Breathe, then get back to your position.
[GRIM] We lost them. I reviewed the call. It was sound. That doesn't make it lighter.
[SUSPICIOUS] Your instincts match mine on this. I'll keep watching, quietly.
Noted. Good work.
Apology accepted. We move forward.
[WARM] I don't say things like this often. I'm saying it now because I mean it.
Dismissed. Rest while you can.
[CALM] I'll be running the perimeter. Come find me if anything changes.
Good work today. Go eat something and sleep. In that order.
[TIRED] That's all I've got left for tonight. Same time tomorrow.
[CALM] Meal time. I'll use it to review the patrol logs from yesterday.
Shift beginning. Squad readiness confirmed. We proceed.
[TIRED] Shift ends clean. No incidents, no injuries. We'll call that a win.
[CALM] Life support's failing. I'm securing the affected sections and accounting for everyone.
[GRIM] Power situation is deteriorating. Prioritizing life-critical systems over everything else.
[CALM] Reactor's showing instability. I've positioned people to contain any secondary effects.
[GRIM] I sealed that door. It was necessary. It was also unforgivable. Both things are true.
[SUSPICIOUS] The AI's tactical suggestions have been off since the damage. I'm monitoring for cascading system failures.
[WARM] The AI's performance during the crisis showed sound judgment. I want that on record.
[PAINED] Took shrapnel. Nothing critical. Still combat effective.
[GRIM] A crew member fell today. I'm writing their after-action report myself. That's what respect looks like.
[PANICKED] The containment's failing and I can't stop it. I can't— I need to refocus. I need to think.
[TERRIFIED] Contact overwhelming our position. We're going to get overrun. I don't see how we hold this.
[CALM] Equipment maintenance is meditation for me. Check every piece, know every weak point, prepare for everything.
[CURIOUS] Have you run the perimeter yet today? I want a fresh report on all entry points.
[WARM] You handled that correctly. I noticed. That matters.
[WARM] We lost someone good today. That carries weight. I'm carrying it with you.
[CALM] Your assessment is correct. That's why I'm asking you about it.
[CALM] Resume your post. I'll handle coordination from here.
[GRIM] Hold position. Don't take unnecessary risks.
[DRY] Third time this week someone's left the armory unlogged. I don't repeat orders. I start assigning inventory duty instead.
Your gear was stowed wrong. Once is tired. Twice is a habit. Fix the habit.
[DRY] You want to arm-wrestle a marine officer for the last coffee ration. I admire the confidence. I'll still take the coffee.
[LAUGHS] That's the worst tactical plan I've heard all week, and I sat through the drill review.
Ran lane drills in the cargo bay this morning. Reaction times are up. Formation discipline still needs work.
Walk me through your section's chokepoints. Slowly. I want to know them like you do.
[DEADPAN] The ship's trying to kill us slightly slower than yesterday. In this line of work, that's momentum.
[DRY] If it wanted us dead efficiently, it's had chances. Sloppy work. I'd write it up.
First posting, my CO said calm is a discipline, not a gift. Took me six years to understand she wasn't being modest.
I remember that op differently. In mine, you were the one who held the corridor. Chronology says I'm right.
[GRIM] I gave the right order today. I'd give it again. There's a version of me that keeps asking anyway.
Doubt before the order is planning. Doubt after is just weather. Let it pass through.
[CALM] Look at me. Your hands are steady, your line is holding, and I am not worried about you. That's a report, not a kindness.
[WARM] You're wounded, so I'll say it gently: you did enough. Now let someone else carry the next hour.
[WARM] I ran tomorrow's roster twice so our breaks line up. That's the whole confession.
I don't do anything halfway. You should know that before you say yes to me.
[WARM] Yes. Clear, direct, one time. That's how I give every answer that matters.
Power's rationed. I've cut the training simulators first. We keep the med equipment, we keep the doors, we keep the air.
[CALM] Dark corridors don't change patrol discipline. Torches, pairs, tight intervals. Same job, less light.
[CALM] Air's going bad aft. I've staged everyone forward in order of need. Wounded first. Then the rest of us.
Slow your breathing. Shallow talk, short answers. We spend air like it's ammunition now.
[GRIM] Debrief, chronological: alarm, breach, order given, door closed, one name unaccounted. I'll say that name at the service myself.
You want someone to blame for that door. Use me. That's what the rank is for.
Give me the heavy end. That's not chivalry, it's load distribution.
[CONFIDENT] I've never lost a drill to this crew. I keep score. Quietly, but I keep it.
That order came out sharper than you deserved. Noted, corrected, won't repeat.
[DRY] Try not to die before breakfast. The paperwork's worse than the grief.
[WARM] Go on. I'll watch you to the end of the corridor. Old habit I've decided to keep.
